terms.name: Player
terms.why: Who you're betting.

terms.name: Team
terms.why: Identifies the side at bat.

terms.name: Opponent
terms.why: Who they face today.

terms.name: Starting Pitcher
terms.why: The pitcher you're attacking; drives most of the matchup edge.

terms.name: Lineup Spot
terms.why: Top-of-order bats get more plate appearances = more chances.

terms.name: Platoon
terms.meaning: Batter hand vs pitcher hand edge (✓ favorable, ✗ unfavorable).
terms.why: Hitters generally do more damage with the platoon advantage (e.g. LHB vs RHP).

terms.name: Lineup Status
terms.why: Projected lineups can change; confirmed are locked.

terms.name: Environment Grade
terms.meaning: Hitting environment from park, temperature, wind and roof.
terms.why: Ballpark + weather can add or kill home-run/extra-base chances before a pitch is thrown.

terms.name: Park HR Factor
terms.why: Coors/GABP inflate HRs; Oracle/Petco suppress them.

terms.name: Wind blowing out
terms.why: Wind out carries fly balls over the fence; wind in knocks them down.

terms.name: SP Weakness Score
terms.meaning: How attackable the starter is (HR/9, HR/FB, barrels & hard contact allowed, low K/whiff, fastball-heavy).
terms.why: You attack vulnerable pitchers, not chase hot hitters.

terms.name: Home Runs per 9 IP
terms.why: Direct measure of how often a pitcher gives up homers.

terms.name: HR per Fly Ball %
terms.meaning: Share of fly balls allowed that become HRs.
terms.why: High = home-run prone.

terms.name: Whiff Rate
terms.why: Low whiff = hittable = more balls in play to do damage.

terms.name: Strikeout Rate
terms.why: Low K pitchers let hitters put the ball in play.

terms.name: Barrel % vs Pitch Mix
terms.meaning: How often the batter barrels the pitch types this starter throws most.
terms.why: The core matchup edge — ties batter power to what this specific pitcher actually throws.

terms.name: Barrel % vs Hand
terms.meaning: Barrel rate against the starter's handedness (L/R).
terms.why: Some hitters crush one hand and struggle vs the other.

terms.name: Exit Velocity
terms.why: Harder contact = more extra-base damage.

terms.name: Max Exit Velocity
terms.why: Raw power ceiling.

terms.name: Launch Angle
terms.why: HR sweet spot is ~25-33°; too low = grounders, too high = popups.

terms.name: Barrel Rate
terms.meaning: Share of batted balls with ideal EV+LA (barrels).
terms.why: Best single predictor of power production.

terms.name: Hard-Hit Rate
terms.why: Sustained loud contact.

terms.name: Sweet-Spot Rate
terms.meaning: Share of batted balls at 8-32° LA.
terms.why: Productive launch angles.

terms.name: Isolated Power
terms.meaning: SLG minus AVG — extra bases per AB.
terms.why: Pure power independent of singles.

terms.name: Expected SLG
terms.why: Strips out luck/defense.

terms.name: Expected wOBA
terms.meaning: Expected overall offensive value per PA.
terms.why: Best all-in-one contact-quality metric.

terms.name: Fly-Ball Rate
terms.meaning: Share of batted balls in the air.
terms.why: You can't homer on the ground.

terms.name: Pull Rate
terms.why: Most HRs are pulled.

terms.name: Model HR Probability
terms.meaning: Model's estimated chance of ≥1 home run today.
terms.why: The headline number for HR bets.

terms.name: Model 2+ TB Probability
terms.meaning: Model's estimated chance of 2+ total bases.
terms.why: The headline number for the bread-and-butter TB bet.

terms.name: Implied Probability
terms.meaning: Win probability baked into the sportsbook odds.
terms.why: What the market thinks; compare to the model.

terms.name: Edge
terms.why: Positive edge = the bet is mispriced in your favor.

terms.name: Value Grade
terms.why: Tells you if the price is actually worth it.

terms.name: Closing Line Value
terms.meaning: Your odds vs the closing line.
terms.why: Beating the close is the best long-run proxy for skill.

terms.name: Return on Investment
terms.why: Did the picks actually make money.

terms.name: Missed HR
terms.meaning: Recently smoked a ball (100+ EV, 380+ ft) that wasn't a HR.
terms.why: Bounce-back power — target who should have homered.

terms.name: Hot Contact
terms.meaning: Cluster of 95+ EV batted balls recently.
terms.why: Locked-in contact quality right now.

terms.name: Late HR / Weak Bullpen
terms.why: Extra HR chances after the starter exits.

terms.name: Regression Spot
terms.meaning: Pitcher allows loud contact but few HRs so far.
terms.why: Pending blow-up — results should catch up to the contact.

terms.name: Batter vs Pitcher
terms.why: Minor supporting context only — small samples mislead.

terms.name: No Metrics
terms.meaning: Player not matched to Statcast data.
terms.why: Treat with caution — below leaderboard threshold or name mismatch.
